segments.s560-g25: چو is the conjunction 'when'. برگشت is a past-tense verb 'returned' (3rd person). بهرام را uses را not as a direct object marker but as a topical/possessive marker here: 'Bahram' is being topicalized as the possessor of the following nouns (روز و بخت). روز و بخت is a fixed poetic pair meaning 'day (good day) and fortune'.
segments.s560-g26: سپرد is a simple past transitive verb 'entrusted/handed over'. به نرسی marks the recipient ('to Nersi'); نرسی here is a proper name (not the verbal senses listed). آن زمان 'that time' is a temporal phrase placed after the verb, and تاج و تخت are the direct objects 'crown and throne'.
segments.s560-g27: چنین است is a simple clause 'so/thus it is'. و links the clauses. این را is the direct-object/topic-marked 'this' (را as object marker). بی‌اندازه دان is an imperative/imperjorative use of دانستن meaning 'know/consider it as immeasurable' (دان here functions as an archaic imperative 'know/consider').
segments.s560-g28: گزاف modifies فلک: together 'گزاف فلک' conveys the idea of a capricious or unreliable heaven/fate (گزاف used in a negative/excessive sense). هر زمان تازه is a quantified temporal phrase 'every time/moment new'; دان again is the imperative 'consider/know'. The whole line advises to regard the heavens (fate) as ever‑changing and always new.
segments.s560-g29: کنون is the adverb 'now'. کار نرسی means 'the affair/deed of Nersi' (classical juxtaposition expressing possession). بگویم is 1st‑person singular present/subjunctive used here as 'I will tell/say'; همی is an archaic verbal particle adding continuous or emphatic aspect (common in Ferdowsi) — together 'کنون کار نرسی بگویم همی' = 'Now I will tell Nersi's affair.'
segments.s560-g30: ز دل is a prepositional phrase 'from the heart' (ز = from). زنگ و زنگار are two related nouns 'rust and tarnish' used metaphorically for sorrow/decay. شویم is 1st‑person plural present/subjunctive 'we become' (here projecting result or emotional change), and همی again gives an archaic continuous/emphatic aspect: 'from the heart we shall become rust and tarnish' (i.e., our hearts will be stained/consumed).
segments.s560-g9: به نیکی is a prepositional phrase (به + noun) meaning “toward/in the direction of goodness.” گراییم is 1st‑person plural hortative/subjunctive (“let us incline/turn”), a poetic form from گراییدن; the following و coordinates to فرمان کنیم, where فرمان is the noun object and کنیم is the 1st‑person plural subjunctive/optative of کردن used to make a compound verbal phrase “let us issue/ make (a) command.”
segments.s560-g10: به داد و دهش is an instrumental/benefactive phrase “by/with justice and giving” (به + nouns). دل گروگان کنیم is a poetic idiom: دل (heart) + گروگان (hostage/pledge) with کنیم (let us make) — literally “let us make the heart a hostage,” i.e. “let us win hearts.” The verb کنیم remains hortative 1st‑person plural.
segments.s560-g11: که here introduces a purpose/result clause (“so that/that”). خوبی و زشتی are balanced nouns “goodness and ugliness/badness.” ز ما is the poetic prepositional phrase “from us” (ز = from), یادگار is the predicate noun “a remembrance/memento,” and the clause sets up what should remain of us (i.e. our deeds).
segments.s560-g12: بماند is a 3rd‑person singular present/subjunctive meaning “may remain / let it remain.” تو جز تخم نیکی is an address to “you” with جز (“except/other than”) + تخم نیکی (“seed of goodness”) — “be nothing but the seed of goodness.” مکار is an admonitory adjective meaning “deceitful”; in classical/poetic Persian the copula (باش) is often omitted, so مکار functions imperatively/ prohibitive in sense “do not be deceitful.”
segments.s560-g13: چو شد is a temporal clause “when it became / when it came to pass.” پادشاهیش is the possessive nominal “his kingship/rule” (پادشاه + ـیش). بر چار ماه is a classical way to indicate duration/point (“upon / at four months” = after four months). The construction gives the time-span of his reign.
segments.s560-g14: This clause is elliptical: the (implied) subject is the same king. برو here has the sense “he went / he departed,” zار (used adverbially/qualifying) conveys wretchedness or bitter lament, and بگریست is the past tense “he wept.” تخت و کلاه are juxtaposed as the objects/concerns of his grief (“throne and cap/crown”); classical style often omits an explicit verb like “left” or uses the verbs of lament to indicate loss.
segments.s560-g15: زمانه is the subject “time / the age.” برین سان is a literary compound meaning “in this manner” (بر + این/ین + سان = on/this + manner). همی is the continuous/adverbial particle “always / continually,” and بگذرد is present/subjunctive/indicative “it passes / will pass.” Together: “Time always passes like this.”
segments.s560-g16: پیش مردم is a prepositional phrase “before/among the people.” آزور (azure/lapis‑lazuli) is adjectival/nominal and here is the thing by which the matter is reckoned or described; بشمرد (from شمردن) is present/future 3rd‑person singular meaning “he/one will count/consider.” In classical usage بشمرد often means “will regard/ reckon (it) as …,” so the clause means “(people) will regard/ count it as (this).”
segments.s560-g1: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. بنشست is a perfective past-3sg verb 'sat down / took his seat' (classical narrative past). The proper name بهرام follows as the subject. بهرامیان is a plural noun 'the Bahramians' (followers/retinue) that will be linked to the verb in the next segment (it is the group affected/assembled).
segments.s560-g2: ببست is a past-tense verb here meaning 'he bound/assembled/ordered' (classical usage can mean 'to bind, to set in order, to assemble'); it continues the action introduced in the previous segment. از پی is an idiomatic sequence meaning 'in pursuit of / for the sake of' (از + پی), where از functions as a preposition and پی marks the notion of pursuit. داد و بخشش are coordinated nouns 'justice and giving/donation', and میان ('among/between') indicates the sphere in which these acts occur.
segments.s560-g3: به here indicates direction/target 'onto/onto his'. تاجش is 'his crown' (تاج + enclitic -ش). زبرجد is the gem 'peridot'. برافشاندند is a past, 3pl verb 'they scattered / they showered (upon)' and agrees with an implied plural subject 'they' (the courtiers/residents).
segments.s560-g4: همی is an emphatic/continuous particle here ('kept / did so / likewise'). نام = 'name'. کرمان names the region. شهش is a compound of 'شاه' with the 3sg suffix '-ش' = 'his king' or better read as 'King of Kerman' in apposition. خواندند is past 3pl 'they called' (i.e. they named him thus).
segments.s560-g5: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (چنین 'thus/so', گفت here functions as the speech verb 'said' — note that the provided lemma senses do not list the verbal sense 'to say', hence the correct sense is not in the given list). کز is the contracted form of 'که از' meaning 'that from / from whom', introducing the source (i.e. from the just God). دادگر is an adjective/noun 'just, just-ruler' and خدای = 'God'.
segments.s560-g6: This is an optative clause expressing a wish/blessing. خرد is the noun 'wisdom'. بادمان is optative: باد here functions as the classical particle 'may it be' combined with the enclitic -مان ('for us' or 'to us'), so 'بادمان' = 'may it be for us / may we have'. بهره 'share/benefit', داد 'justice', and رای 'counsel/opinion' are coordinated objects of that optative. The conjunctions و simply link the nouns.
segments.s560-g7: This short clause contains a noun phrase plus the verb 'nماند' 'did not remain'. سرای normally means 'house' or 'dwelling' here but the provided lemma senses list verbs (likely a mismatch), so the appropriate sense is not in the provided list. سپنجی is an adjectival qualifier (textual/lexical difficulty: the manuscript/lemma list gives 'sponge' but the precise poetic sense is obscure); together they form a noun phrase 'the ... house'. نماند is past negative 'did not remain / was left not' and به کس = 'for anyone' ('to/for anyone').
segments.s560-g8: ترا is a fused sequence meaning 'to you' (here shown as ت + را in the word list; ت is the second-person marker and را functions as the object/benefactive marker). نیکوی is an adjective 'good/excellent'. باد is again the optative particle 'may it be', and فریادرس is a noun 'rescuer / succourer'. The whole is a blessing: 'May a good rescuer be (to) you.'
segments.s560-g17: Imperative clause with object first: می‌لعل (a poetic compound 'mey‑lal') is the thing being commanded — here 'ruby‑red wine'. پیش آور is the imperative phrase 'bring before' (پیش + آور 'bring'); the vocative ای attached to آور (آور‌ای) addresses روزبه (O Rouzbeh). Word order puts the object (می‌لعل) and the prepositional phrase (پیش) before the verb, a common older/poetic option.
segments.s560-g18: Temporal subordinate clause introduced by چو 'when'. شد is the past tense 'became/was' and governs the predicate; سال گوینده is a genitive phrase 'the year of the speaker' meaning 'the speaker's years/age'. بر + numeral expresses attaining a quantity/point in older Persian ('reached'); شست و سه is the classical numeral 'sixty‑three' (شست is an archaic form for sixty) with و linking the units.
segments.s560-g19: Another temporal clause: چو 'when' + subject بهرام and past verb دانست 'he knew/realized'. کامدش is an archaic verbal form meaning 'came/was coming', with the enclitic ‑ش 'to him' (showing that what is coming affects him). مرگ is the substantive 'death' and functions as the subject of کامدش in this older SOV order ('death came to him').
segments.s560-g20: Rhetorical/interrogative clause: نهنگی is an indefinite/qualifying form 'a leviathan/a giant' (the suffix ‑ی here often marks an instance or type); کجا is the interrogative 'where/how'; بشکرد is an archaic verbal form (here used in the sense 'would come to pass / appear') and governs the following coordinated objects پیل و کرگ 'elephant and karg'. The line reads as a rhetorical question about where so great a monster would show itself to affect even elephant and karg.
segments.s560-g21: Simple past clause: جهان را marks the definite direct object with را before the verb; به فرزند بسپرد is a dative phrase 'entrusted to his son' (بسپرد is the past form 'entrusted/handed over'). The sequence بسپرد و گفت links two past actions ('he entrusted ... and said').
segments.s560-g22: Reported speech introduced by که 'that'. با مهتران is a prepositional phrase 'with the chiefs/elders' (مهتران = plural of مهتر, here 'chiefs'); آفرین باد is an optative/formulaic expression where آفرین ('praise'/'acclamation') is followed by باد, the optative particle meaning 'may it be' — together 'may there be praise'. جفت 'pair/mate' here is the noun governed by the preceding preposition phrase: the wish is that he be 'paired' or 'joined' with the chiefs and called praiseworthy.
segments.s560-g23: A string of imperatives coordinated by و. Each verb is second‑person imperative in classical/poetic form (the prefixed ب as in بنوش, بباز, بناز, ببخش). The verbs sequence actions/admonitions ('drink', 'play/gamble or 'open (one's hand)', 'be proud/show off', 'grant/forgive'), and the repeated و simply coordinates them into a single exhortation.
segments.s560-g24: Negative imperative begun with مکن 'do not'. روز here is a temporal or emphatic noun 'a day/any day'; بر + noun phrases (بر تاج و بر تخت) indicate 'upon the crown and upon the throne'. دخش is a noun 'radiance/splendour' (or possibly a name), and in this phrase it qualifies the throne (the throne's splendour). Together the clause warns: 'do not let any day be upon (i.e. bring [shame or change to]) the crown and the throne's splendour.'

sentences.s560-s13: When Bahram's day and fortune returned, he handed the crown and throne over to Nersi at that time.
sentences.s560-s14: So it is; regard this as boundless — consider the fickle heavens ever‑new at every moment.
sentences.s560-s15: Now I will tell the affair of Nersi; from the heart we shall become rust and tarnish.
sentences.s560-s5: Let us turn toward goodness and issue commands; let us win hearts by justice and generosity.
sentences.s560-s6: so that our goodness and badness remain as a memorial; you, be nothing but a seed of goodness — do not be deceitful.
sentences.s560-s7: When his kingship reached four months, he went away wretched and bewailed and wept over throne and crown.
sentences.s560-s8: Time will always pass in this way; people will judge/consider it (so) before others.
sentences.s560-s1: When Bahram took his seat, he gathered the Bahramians and set out in pursuit of justice and generosity among them.
sentences.s560-s2: They showered his crown with peridots, and they called him the King of Kerman.
sentences.s560-s3: He said thus: 'May one just God grant us wisdom, benefit, justice, and counsel.'
sentences.s560-s4: No humble dwelling remained for anyone; may a good rescuer be yours.
sentences.s560-s9: Bring the ruby‑wine before me, O Rouzbeh; for when the speaker's years reached sixty‑three.
sentences.s560-s10: When Bahram realized that death was approaching him — where would a leviathan appear to strike down elephant and karg?
sentences.s560-s11: He handed the world over to his son and said, 'May he be joined with the chiefs and receive acclaim.'
sentences.s560-s12: Drink, gamble, swagger, and give; but do not let any day fall upon the crown and the throne's splendour.
